items.title: Automating Formal Verification with Reinforcement Learning and Recursive Inference
items.abstract: Automated formal verification remains challenging for large language models because data for proof assistants and verification-aware languages is scarce, and correctness depends on satisfying precise machine-checkable specifications rather than producing plausible code. This thesis studies how verifier environments can improve LLM generation of verified programs and proofs through reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) and verifier-guided inference-time search. First, we train open-source models in Dafny with RLVR using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and related variants, assembling generated candidates into complete programs and scoring them with compiler and verifier outcomes. Initial experiments on an APPS-derived Dafny dataset increased verified reward from 2.2% to 58.1%, but revealed specification hacking, where models exploit weak formal specifications instead of implementing the intended solutions. After filtering underspecified and vulnerable tasks, multi-turn RLVR on the refined benchmark improves the verified pass rate from 9.7% to 31.1%. Second, we develop a verifier-guided inference scaffold in Lean that treats proof generation as structured search over decomposed subgoals, verifier feedback, diagnostics, and repair. With a fixed base model, the full scaffold with proof reviser improves pass rate on an initial VeriCoding pilot set from 46.2% under direct repair to 69.2%. On the larger VERINA dataset, whole-task decomposition plus proof reviser solves 7 of 42 previously unsolved tasks. We also introduce Dalek-Bench, a repository-scale Lean benchmark derived from the Rust $\texttt{curve25519-dalek}$ verification project; preliminary results remain weak, indicating that stronger progress evaluation and task-specific tool-use policies are still needed.
items.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards for formal verification
items.llm_evidence_cn: 利用可验证奖励的强化学习来自动化形式验证
items.llm_evidence: 利用可验证奖励的强化学习来自动化形式验证
items.canonical_evidence: 利用可验证奖励的强化学习来自动化形式验证
items.llm_tldr_en: This thesis applies RLVR and verifier-guided inference-time search with LLMs to automate formal verification in Dafny, showing improved generation of verified programs.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大语言模型在形式验证中数据稀缺的问题，通过可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）和验证器引导的推理搜索，在Dafny语言上训练开源模型生成经过验证的程序和证明，初步实验表明该方法能有效提升验证成功率，为自动化形式验证提供了新思路。
items.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大语言模型在形式验证中数据稀缺的问题，通过可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）和验证器引导的推理搜索，在Dafny语言上训练开源模型生成经过验证的程序和证明，初步实验表明该方法能有效提升验证成功率，为自动化形式验证提供了新思路。
